summary: Closed, unmerged PR that attempted to decompile and 100% match three stage material functions: grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, and grMaterial_801C9698. The patch corrected grMaterial_801C9470/801C9490 to use Item_GObj and CommandInfo, implemented ColorOverlay handling via Ground-relative pointer arithmetic, and added the needed lb forward include. A later comment explained the pointer arithmetic was not easily replaceable with struct fields because the material system overlays ColorOverlay data at Ground+0x40 and uses padding around Ground+0xC0. The PR was later closed with the note that it was consolidated into other PRs.

key_files.role: Implemented grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, grMaterial_801C9698 and adjusted grMaterial_801C9470 forwarding/signature.
Added lb/forward.h and updated grMaterial_801C9470 prototype from HSD_JObj* to Item_GObj*, CommandInfo*.

what_changed: Replaced the grMaterial_801C9490 stub with code taking Item_GObj* and CommandInfo*, reading a u16 command value from cmd->ptr[0], extracting ((value >> 2) & 0xFF), storing it as f32 at Ground+0xC0, and setting gp->x10_flags.b6.
Changed grMaterial_801C9470 from an HSD_JObj* wrapper to an Item_GObj*/CommandInfo* wrapper that forwards to grMaterial_801C9490.
Implemented grMaterial_801C9604 to treat Ground+0x40 as a ColorOverlay, initialize priority/pointer/timer/loop/color-enable fields, clear gp->x10_flags.b4, then call grMaterial_801C9698.
Implemented grMaterial_801C9698 to call lb_80014258(gobj, ColorOverlay at Ground+0x40, fn_801C9664) and set gp->x10_flags.b4 if that helper returns true.
Added lb/forward.h to grmaterial.h, apparently to support the CommandInfo prototype.
PR body claimed ninja built cleanly and fuzzy_match_percent was 100.0 for all three decompiled functions.
The PR was closed unmerged after a comment saying the work was consolidated into other PRs.
smart_moves: Corrected the callback-style signatures to Item_GObj* and CommandInfo* instead of forcing the old HSD_JObj* declaration, which better matched the command dispatch code.
Used the existing ColorOverlay type for the Ground+0x40 overlay rather than leaving all accesses as raw bytes.
Kept explicit byte-offset pointer arithmetic where the shared Ground struct did not accurately model the material overlay layout, avoiding speculative struct fields.
Documented why the ugly offsets were present: ColorOverlay occupies an overlaid Ground region and Ground+0xC0 lies in unnamed padding before GroundVars.
Preserved flag behavior around gp->x10_flags.b4 and gp->x10_flags.b6 while matching the helper/callback flow through lb_80014258 and fn_801C9664.
decomp_lessons: Stage material code may overlay temporary material data onto generic Ground storage; do not add naive Ground fields until the overlapping layout is understood.
Pointer arithmetic such as (u8*) gp + 0x40 or +0xC0 can be the least-wrong interim representation when a shared struct needs a future union to model overlays.
When an item-command callback function appears to take the wrong object type, check the ItCmd/CommandInfo dispatch path before preserving an old placeholder prototype.
A 100% fuzzy match claim is useful evidence, but a closed-unmerged PR should be treated as a source of tactics, not necessarily final project state.
For future cleanup, the comment points to a possible Ground union covering roughly offsets 0x40 through 0xC3 to represent the ColorOverlay/padding overlap.
naming_conventions: Address-suffixed grMaterial names were retained: grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, grMaterial_801C9698.
The patch used short local names consistent with nearby decomp style: gobj for HSD_GObj/Item_GObj, gp for Ground*, co for ColorOverlay*, and cmd for CommandInfo*.
Existing offset-based field names were reused rather than renamed: x10_flags.b4, x10_flags.b6, x4_pri, x8_ptr1, x0_timer, xC_loop, x7C_color_enable, x7C_flag2.
The file/module remains lowercase grmaterial while functions use the existing grMaterial_ address-prefix style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Command bit extraction in grMaterial_801C9490 used (*(u16*) cmd->ptr[0] >> 2) & 0xFF, then converted the result to f32 for storage.
Ground-relative casts were used to force exact layout: (ColorOverlay*) ((u8*) gp + 0x40) and *(f32*) ((u8*) gp + 0xC0).
grMaterial_801C9604 zeroed multiple ColorOverlay state fields before immediately calling grMaterial_801C9698, matching the init-then-update control flow.
grMaterial_801C9698 used the boolean return from lb_80014258 to conditionally set gp->x10_flags.b4.
Verification evidence in the PR body was limited to a clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for the three functions.

review_feedback.feedback: Author initially said pointer math would be fixed soon.
review_feedback.resolution: A later comment said the pointer math could not simply become struct field access because ColorOverlay is overlaid at Ground+0x40 and Ground+0xC0 is unnamed padding.

review_feedback.feedback: Fixing the layout properly would require adding a union around the Ground 0x40-0xC3 region.
review_feedback.resolution: Marked as non-trivial; no such union is shown in this PR slice.

review_feedback.feedback: The PR was consolidated into other PRs.

follow_up_queries: Find the later consolidated PRs that replaced PR 2249 for grMaterial_801C9490 grMaterial_801C9604 grMaterial_801C9698.
Check current master signatures for grMaterial_801C9470 and grMaterial_801C9490.
Search for Ground struct overlay or union work around offsets 0x40 through 0xC3.
Search for other uses of lb_80014258 with ColorOverlay and fn_801C9664.
Verify whether pointer arithmetic at Ground+0x40 and Ground+0xC0 remains in the final accepted grmaterial implementation.
